what_it_does: Documents how the Company runtime is started, stopped, monitored, and restored: the `company` CLI, the self-describing service registry, systemd unit conventions, the on-demand boot policy, GPU VRAM budgeting, mobile Tailscale access, scheduled transcript-search jobs, and the limits of what the repo can recreate.
observations: The `company` CLI is the single console for status, up/down, logs, GPU, health, models, swap, and bench.
No user services auto-start at boot; only `ollama` and `tailscaled` remain system-boot services.
Services are described in `ops/services.json` and driven by systemd underneath.
`company status` groups services and shows live state plus a 16 GB GPU VRAM budget line.
`company up <model>` warns if the start would exceed 16 GB but does not yet hard-refuse.
`company swap` is generic but refuses `embed-jina-v5` and `embed-qwen3` until their scripts are normalized.
Jobs are systemd timers bound to `company.target` so they rise/fall with the Company, not enabled at boot.
The interim transcript-search circuit (exporter + reindex) is explicitly labeled throwaway pending the real memory system.
Mobile access works via Tailscale serve and a PWA named Vi.
Canonical unit files live in `ops/systemd/`; the repo honestly flags model weights/venvs and legacy scripts as not carried.
standouts: Deliberate no-auto-start boot policy to avoid 16 GB VRAM contention.
`company` CLI as the single brain replacing the old `vllm-stack` tool.
VRAM budgeting with overcommit warnings built into the operator workflow.
Established iPhone access path via Tailscale HTTPS PWA.
Honest documentation of out-of-repo dependencies and unfinished migrations.
conventions: Self-describing JSON registry (`ops/services.json`) for service metadata.
Single CLI wrapper over systemd user units.
Canonical unit files stored in repo under `ops/systemd/`.
Delta, marker-gated scheduled jobs to avoid unnecessary GPU use.
Honest `What the repo does NOT carry` section.
concerns: VRAM overcommit is only warned, not enforced (`open decision`).
`company swap` cannot handle two embed services due to unnormalized scripts.
Interim transcript-search circuit is temporary and will be replaced.
Legacy serve scripts outside the repo are still required by some services.
Five voice trial engines have no unit files yet.
WSL boot reachability depends on a Windows Task Scheduler step not in the repo.
notes: This file is the human runbook and boot contract for the Company stack: read it before starting, stopping, or restoring anything.
questions: What is the exact schema and location of `ops/services.json`?
Which services are in each group (core/brain/voice/models/reach/jobs)?
How is per-service VRAM cost measured and kept accurate?
Where is the `company` CLI implemented and what commands does it actually parse?
What is the real memory system that will replace the interim transcript-search circuit?
What are the five voice trial engines and how are they installed?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is the single starting point for startup and control of the Company runtime.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth in repo for units and registry.
Mobile remote access as a first-class path.
intention_signals: Make a personal/local AI lab manageable through one CLI, avoid resource contention by never auto-starting services, provide a clear restore path, and be transparent about what the repo cannot recreate.
novelty: A home-grown `company` CLI wrapping systemd with a 16 GB VRAM budget warning, an explicit boot-disable policy, and a documented throwaway interim search pipeline.
contribution: Provides the operational contract and human runbook for starting, stopping, monitoring, and restoring the Company stack; defines the on-demand boot policy, CLI surface, service registry conventions, mobile access path, and scheduled job beats that a fused system would need to preserve.
summary_for_embedding: company/ops/STARTUP.md is the operator runbook documenting the `company` CLI, on-demand systemd service orchestration, VRAM budgeting, mobile Tailscale access, scheduled transcript-search jobs, and repo-restore limits for the Company stack.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give Tim a single, reproducible control point for bringing up only the services a task needs while staying within GPU memory and keeping the repo honest about what it can restore.
